questions.stem: This postmortem liver specimen contains multiple flat, leaf-shaped parasitic organisms within the parenchyma and biliary tree. To which class of parasite do these organisms belong?

explanation.summary: The flat, leaf-shaped worms shown are liver flukes, members of the class Trematoda (phylum Platyhelminthes). Examples that invade the biliary tree include Fasciola hepatica, Clonorchis sinensis, and Opisthorchis species.
explanation.whyCorrect: The flat, leaf-shaped worms shown are liver flukes, members of the class Trematoda (phylum Platyhelminthes). Examples that invade the biliary tree include Fasciola hepatica, Clonorchis sinensis, and Opisthorchis species.
